market_session: US regular session close on 2026-06-22 (first session after the Juneteenth three-day weekend)

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from AP closing-index ledger; prior session was 2026-06-18 because 2026-06-19 was the Juneteenth holiday

text: US stocks finished mixed on Monday, June 22, 2026. The S&P 500 fell 27.79 points (0.4%) to 7,472.79; the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 148.01 points (0.3%) to 51,712.71; the Nasdaq Composite fell 351.33 points (1.3%) to 26,166.60; and the Russell 2000 rose 24.64 points (0.8%) to 3,004.40.
At Monday's close the S&P 500 stood 1.8% below its all-time high, which was set early in June 2026.
The small-cap Russell 2000 rose 0.8% to 3,004.40, closing above the 3,000 level for the first time ever.

value.oil_driver: US-Iran negotiations on ending their war
value.reopen: first session after a three-day Juneteenth weekend

text: Stocks drifted through a mixed session as oil prices eased and falling Big Tech shares weighed on the market. Oil fell roughly 3% following negotiations between the United States and Iran on ending their war, while rising Treasury yields in the bond market also pressured equities. Trading resumed after a three-day weekend for the Juneteenth holiday.

value.alphabet_driver: concerns over AI-talent departures to competitors

text: Mega-cap technology shares led the Nasdaq lower. Per Yahoo Finance's session wrap, Alphabet fell about 5% amid concerns over AI-talent departures to competitors, and Amazon, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms also closed lower.

value.development: reported encouraging progress toward ending the US-Iran conflict
value.roadmap: committee reported to have agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days

text: Investors weighed reports of encouraging progress in negotiations aimed at ending the US-Iran conflict, with a high-level committee reported to have agreed to a roadmap toward a final deal within 60 days after weekend talks in Switzerland. The diplomatic progress coincided with lower oil prices.
